title: Guarea grandifolia
intro: Guarea grandifolia, also known as the Guarea tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Meliaceae, native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The Guarea tree has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Guarea grandifolia include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenes, such as guarein and guareenin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The Guarea tree is commonly used to prepare teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Guarea grandifolia belongs to the family Meliaceae. This family comprises about 300 species of trees and shrubs, commonly known as the mahogany family. The genus Guarea is a group of tropical trees native to the Americas and Africa. Guarea grandifolia is one of the species within this genus, characterized by its large leaves and distinctive bark. Its classification is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, and class Magnoliopsida.
uses: Guarea grandifolia has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. Its bark and leaves are often employed in traditional medicine to treat fever, rheumatism, and other ailments. In some regions, it is used to make tea that is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is also used in folk medicine to treat digestive issues and as a treatment for snake bites. Further research is needed to fully understand the extent of its medicinal uses and potential benefits.

preparations: Guarea grandifolia is a species of tree native to the tropical regions of Central and South America.  Herbal preparations from Guarea grandifolia have been used by indigenous communities for various purposes, including medicinal and ritualistic uses.
